myersBriggs.name:
INTJ
(Introversion, Intuition, Thinking, Judging)
myersBriggs.description: Strategic, independent, and determined. Excels in long-term planning and innovation but can be private and perfectionistic.

myersBriggs.name:
INTP
(Introversion, Intuition, Thinking, Perceiving)
myersBriggs.description: Intellectual, curious, and analytical. Loves exploring abstract concepts and theories but can struggle with follow-through.

myersBriggs.name:
ENTJ
(Extroversion, Intuition, Thinking, Judging)
myersBriggs.description: Bold, decisive, and efficient leader. Thrives in structured environments and is driven to achieve big goals but may be seen as intimidating.

myersBriggs.name:
ENTP
(Extroversion, Intuition, Thinking, Perceiving)
myersBriggs.description: Witty, energetic, and argumentative. Enjoys mental sparring and pushing boundaries but can struggle with routine and follow-through.

myersBriggs.name:
INFJ
(Introversion, Intuition, Feeling, Judging)
myersBriggs.description: Deep, introspective, and highly empathetic. Has a strong sense of purpose and often works toward helping others but may be prone to burnout.

myersBriggs.name:
INFP
(Introversion, Intuition, Feeling, Perceiving)
myersBriggs.description: Idealistic, creative, and deeply emotional. Seeks meaning in everything but can be overly sensitive and struggle with practical matters.

myersBriggs.name:
ENFJ
(Extroversion, Intuition, Feeling, Judging)
myersBriggs.description: Charismatic, inspiring, and driven by the desire to help others. A natural leader but may take on too much responsibility.

myersBriggs.name:
ENFP
(Extroversion, Intuition, Feeling, Perceiving)
myersBriggs.description: Enthusiastic, spontaneous, and highly creative. Loves exploring possibilities but may struggle with structure and follow-through.

myersBriggs.name:
ISTJ
(Introversion, Sensing, Thinking, Judging)
myersBriggs.description: Responsible, disciplined, and detail-oriented. Values tradition and reliability but can be rigid and resistant to change.

myersBriggs.name:
ISFJ
(Introversion, Sensing, Feeling, Judging)
myersBriggs.description: Warm, nurturing, and dependable. Loyal and hard-working but may struggle with self-advocacy.

myersBriggs.name:
ESTJ
(Extroversion, Sensing, Thinking, Judging)
myersBriggs.description: Organized, practical, and strong-willed. Thrives in leadership roles but may come across as too controlling or blunt.

myersBriggs.name:
ESFJ
(Extroversion, Sensing, Feeling, Judging)
myersBriggs.description: Social, caring, and community-focused. Excels in fostering harmony but may struggle with criticism or change.

myersBriggs.name:
ISTP
(Introversion, Sensing, Thinking, Perceiving)
myersBriggs.description: Independent, hands-on, and resourceful. Loves working with tools and solving practical problems but can be impulsive or distant.

myersBriggs.name:
ISFP
(Introversion, Sensing, Feeling, Perceiving)
myersBriggs.description: Artistic, free-spirited, and highly in tune with emotions. Craves new experiences but may struggle with long-term commitments.

myersBriggs.name:
ESTP
(Extroversion, Sensing, Thinking, Perceiving)
myersBriggs.description: Energetic, action-oriented, and charming. Loves excitement and risk-taking but may struggle with long-term planning.

myersBriggs.name:
ESFP
(Extroversion, Sensing, Feeling, Perceiving)
myersBriggs.description: Fun-loving, social, and spontaneous. Thrives in the moment but may avoid deeper or serious issues.

DISC.name: Dominance (D)
DISC.title: The Leader
DISC.description: Bold, assertive, and results-driven. Thrives in leadership roles and embraces challenges but can be impatient or overly aggressive.

DISC.name: Influence (I)
DISC.title: The Persuader
DISC.description: Charismatic, social, and enthusiastic. Great at communication and building relationships but can be disorganized or easily distracted.

DISC.name: Steadiness (S)
DISC.title: The Supporter
DISC.description: Loyal, patient, and dependable. A great team player who values harmony but may struggle with change or setting boundaries.

DISC.name: Conscientiousness (C)
DISC.title: The Analyst
DISC.description: Detail-oriented, analytical, and precise. Values accuracy and structure but may be overly critical or struggle with spontaneity.

enneagram.name: Type 1: The Reformer
enneagram.description: Principled, disciplined, and perfectionistic. Strives for integrity and high standards but can be overly critical and rigid.

enneagram.name: Type 2: The Helper
enneagram.description: Warm, caring, and generous. Seeks to support others but may struggle with setting boundaries and seeking validation.

enneagram.name: Type 3: The Achiever
enneagram.description: Ambitious, adaptable, and success-driven. Excels at goal-setting but may be overly focused on image and external validation.

enneagram.name: Type 4: The Individualist
enneagram.description: Creative, introspective, and deeply emotional. Values authenticity but may struggle with mood swings and feeling misunderstood.

enneagram.name: Type 5: The Investigator
enneagram.description: Analytical, curious, and independent. Loves knowledge and deep thinking but may be emotionally detached or withdrawn.

enneagram.name: Type 6: The Loyalist
enneagram.description: Responsible, trustworthy, and security-oriented. Values stability and preparedness but can be anxious or skeptical.

enneagram.name: Type 7: The Enthusiast
enneagram.description: Fun-loving, spontaneous, and adventurous. Seeks excitement and new experiences but may struggle with commitment and focus.

enneagram.name: Type 8: The Challenger
enneagram.description: Confident, assertive, and protective. A strong leader who values control but can be confrontational or overly domineering.

enneagram.name: Type 9: The Peacemaker
enneagram.description: Easygoing, empathetic, and harmonious. Avoids conflict and seeks peace but may struggle with indecisiveness and passivity.

big5.name: Openness
big5.description: Characterized by imagination, curiosity, and a preference for novelty and variety. People high in openness are creative and open to new experiences, while those low in openness prefer routine and tradition.

big5.name: Conscientiousness
big5.description: Reflects a person's level of organization, dependability, and self-discipline. Highly conscientious individuals are responsible and goal-oriented, while lower conscientiousness may indicate spontaneity or a lack of structure.

big5.name: Extraversion
big5.description: Associated with sociability, assertiveness, and enthusiasm. Extroverts enjoy social interactions and stimulation, while introverts prefer solitude and quieter environments.

big5.name: Agreeableness
big5.description: Relates to compassion, cooperation, and social harmony. Highly agreeable people are empathetic and kind, while those lower in agreeableness may be more competitive or skeptical.

big5.name: Neuroticism
big5.description: Indicates emotional stability and resilience. High neuroticism is linked to anxiety, mood swings, and stress sensitivity, while low neuroticism is associated with calmness and emotional stability.

hollandCode.name: Realistic
hollandCode.description: Hands-on, practical, and mechanically inclined. Prefers working with tools, machines, and the outdoors. Enjoys physical tasks and problem-solving through direct action.

hollandCode.name: Investigative
hollandCode.description: Curious, analytical, and intellectual. Enjoys research, problem-solving, and exploring abstract concepts. Prefers working with ideas rather than people or things.

hollandCode.name: Artistic
hollandCode.description: Creative, expressive, and imaginative. Prefers unstructured environments that allow for self-expression through art, music, writing, or design.

hollandCode.name: Social
hollandCode.description: Empathetic, cooperative, and people-oriented. Enjoys helping, teaching, and supporting others. Prefers roles that involve direct interaction and teamwork.

hollandCode.name: Enterprising
hollandCode.description: Persuasive, ambitious, and energetic. Enjoys leadership, business, and taking risks. Prefers roles involving sales, management, or entrepreneurship.

hollandCode.name: Conventional
hollandCode.description: Organized, detail-oriented, and structured. Prefers working with data, numbers, and systematic tasks. Enjoys roles involving administration, finance, or clerical work.
